# Omega Guard

> Omega Guard is a brand-protection and consumer-protection service that gives a business a verified trust mark customers can check in one click, and continuously monitors the web for clone and impostor websites that copy the brand. When a fake is confirmed by a human reviewer, Omega Guard preserves the evidence and supplies a ready-to-file takedown packet. It is a product of Omega Point Solutions LLC, a Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Business (SDVOSB).

Primary site: https://omega-guard.omegapointsolutions.com/
Verify any business (free): https://guard.omegapointsolutions.com/
Parent company: https://omegapointsolutions.com/

## What Omega Guard does

Omega Guard has two pillars:

1. **Verified Business identity + public trust mark.** A business proves control of its domain and Omega Guard fingerprints the authentic site. Consumers can then check any domain for free and see whether it is a verified member, unknown, or a reported impostor — before they pay, book, or share data.

2. **Active clone and impostor detection.** Omega Guard continuously sweeps the public web for lookalike domains, newly registered copycats, and cloned pages spoofing a member's brand. Confirmed fakes come with preserved evidence and a takedown packet.

## How detection works (signals fused)

- Lookalike-domain permutation analysis (typo/swap/homoglyph domains)
- Certificate-transparency (CT) log monitoring for newly issued certificates
- Favicon hashing (MurmurHash3)
- Structural HTML fuzzy-hashing (simhash) for page-structure similarity
- Screenshot-level visual similarity (perceptual hashing)
- Domain age and registrar via RDAP
- Domain-control verification (DNS-TXT)
- Hosting IP / ASN reputation

Every candidate is scored, then confirmed by a trained human reviewer before any action. Software assists; it never acts autonomously.

## What a member receives when a fake is confirmed

- An alert
- A preserved evidence bundle: page capture, WHOIS, certificate, and archive — each hashed with SHA-256 for chain of custody
- A structured, ready-to-send takedown packet for the registrar and host
- A member dashboard and ongoing monitoring

## Who it is for

Small and mid-sized businesses whose customers trust them with money and data: medical and dental practices, insurance agencies and carriers, auto dealers, financial and title services, real estate, home-services businesses, and retailers — especially those without an in-house security team. Impersonation is the most-reported type of cybercrime to the FBI's Internet Crime Complaint Center (IC3).

## Privacy and ethics

Omega Guard is privacy-first by design. It analyzes impostor website artifacts, not people, and monitors only on behalf of enrolled, consenting members. It is not a surveillance, profiling, or biometric system, and it does not build profiles of individuals. A human reviews and decides before anything is acted on, every action is logged, and AI involvement is always disclosed.

## Key facts

- Product name: Omega Guard (trademark of Omega Point Solutions LLC)
- Category: Brand protection, clone-site / website-impersonation detection, business verification
- Founder: Cody Rose
- Public verification: free, no account required, at guard.omegapointsolutions.com
- Web sweep cadence: every 30 minutes for verified members
- Review model: human-in-the-loop on every confirmation
- Provider: Omega Point Solutions LLC (SDVOSB), https://omegapointsolutions.com

## Resources

- [What is a clone website?](https://omega-guard.omegapointsolutions.com/what-is-a-clone-website): How fake sites copy a brand, how to spot one, and how clone-site detection works — with a comparison of defense options.
- [How to get a fake or clone website taken down](https://omega-guard.omegapointsolutions.com/how-to-take-down-a-fake-website): A step-by-step guide to preserving evidence, finding the registrar and host, and filing takedown complaints.
- [Brand impersonation and imposter-scam statistics (2026)](https://omega-guard.omegapointsolutions.com/brand-impersonation-statistics-2026): Sourced figures from the FTC and FBI IC3 on imposter-scam losses and complaint volumes.
